document.title: March 16, 2023 Meeting of the Antimicrobial Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document

key_concerns: Uncertain efficacy in vaccinated or previously infected individuals
Efficacy against Omicron variant not directly studied
Limited data in immunocompromised patients
COVID-19 rebound incidence and mechanism unclear
Risk of serious adverse reactions due to drug-drug interactions
key_positives: Significant reduction in hospitalization/death in unvaccinated high-risk adults (EPIC-HR)
Oral administration provides an accessible treatment option
Acceptable safety profile with mostly mild adverse events
Already authorized under EUA, suggesting prior positive benefit-risk assessment

key_concerns: Uncertain efficacy in vaccinated or previously infected individuals
Efficacy against Omicron variant not directly studied
Limited data in immunocompromised patients
COVID-19 rebound incidence and mechanism unclear
Risk of serious adverse reactions due to drug-drug interactions
key_positives: Significant reduction in hospitalization/death in unvaccinated high-risk adults (EPIC-HR)
Oral administration provides an accessible treatment option
Acceptable safety profile with mostly mild adverse events
Already authorized under EUA, suggesting prior positive benefit-risk assessment
evidence_notes: EPIC-HR demonstrated a 5.6% absolute reduction... 86% relative reduction — strong efficacy signal in original trial population
EPIC-SR failed to demonstrate any meaningful difference for the primary efficacy endpoint — lack of symptom alleviation benefit in broader population
included very few immunocompromised subjects — insufficient evidence in immunocompromised
excluded individuals with current or expected use of any medications that have DDIs — safety data limited due to DDI exclusions
a numerically lower rate of COVID-19-related hospitalizations or deaths... in the subgroup of vaccinated high-risk subjects — suggestive but non-significant benefit in vaccinated high-risk

signal.label: STRONG_POSITIVE
rationale: Concerns: Uncertain efficacy in vaccinated or previously infected individuals; Efficacy against Omicron variant not directly studied; Limited data in immunocompromised patients; COVID-19 rebound incidence and mechanism unclear; Risk of serious adverse reactions due to drug-drug interactions

Positives: Significant reduction in hospitalization/death in unvaccinated high-risk adults (EPIC-HR); Oral administration provides an accessible treatment option; Acceptable safety profile with mostly mild adverse events; Already authorized under EUA, suggesting prior positive benefit-risk assessment

history_rank.title: March 16, 2023 Meeting of the Antimicrobial Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document

document.title: April 17, 2023 Meeting of the Antimicrobial Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document

key_concerns: Small safety database (N=158 SUL-DUR exposed)
Single pivotal trial with 125 subjects in primary analysis
Wide non-inferiority margin of 20%
Imbalances in baseline characteristics (age, race, region) raising potential confounding
Limited U.S. enrollment (1 subject) affecting generalizability
No multiplicity adjustment for secondary efficacy endpoints
No independent adjudication committee for endpoints
Potential impact of COVID-19 on enrollment and trial conduct
key_positives: Primary endpoint met: SUL-DUR noninferior to colistin for 28-day mortality
Upper bound of 95% CI (3.5%) well below 20% margin, would satisfy a 10% margin
Numerically lower mortality (19% vs 32.3%) and favorable secondary endpoints
High unmet medical need for CRABC with limited treatment options
No unexpected safety signals identified in development program
Consistent efficacy across analysis populations and supportive nonclinical data

key_concerns: Small safety database (N=158 SUL-DUR exposed)
Single pivotal trial with 125 subjects in primary analysis
Wide non-inferiority margin of 20%
Imbalances in baseline characteristics (age, race, region) raising potential confounding
Limited U.S. enrollment (1 subject) affecting generalizability
No multiplicity adjustment for secondary efficacy endpoints
No independent adjudication committee for endpoints
Potential impact of COVID-19 on enrollment and trial conduct
key_positives: Primary endpoint met: SUL-DUR noninferior to colistin for 28-day mortality
Upper bound of 95% CI (3.5%) well below 20% margin, would satisfy a 10% margin
Numerically lower mortality (19% vs 32.3%) and favorable secondary endpoints
High unmet medical need for CRABC with limited treatment options
No unexpected safety signals identified in development program
Consistent efficacy across analysis populations and supportive nonclinical data
evidence_notes: Primary analysis: 'SUL-DUR was noninferior to colistin using a 20% NI margin' — upper 95% CI 3.5%.
Safety limitation: 'the limited size of the current safety database for SUL-DUR' — only 158 subjects exposed.
Subgroup robustness: 'The sample sizes for many subgroups were small, which limits the ability to identify trends with certainty.'
Multiplicity concern: 'The Applicant did not specify how to control multiplicity for testing the secondary efficacy endpoints.'
Baseline imbalance noted: '10% differences in ... age, race, enrollment area and infection type' between arms.
US representativeness: 'Only one (0.8%) subject was enrolled in the United States.'

questions_framing.description: FDA asks committee to assess benefit-risk given unmet need and limited data, with conditional framing ('If yes... If no...') that does not telegraph a negative recommendation.

signal.label: STRONG_POSITIVE
rationale: Concerns: Small safety database (N=158 SUL-DUR exposed); Single pivotal trial with 125 subjects in primary analysis; Wide non-inferiority margin of 20%; Imbalances in baseline characteristics (age, race, region) raising potential confounding; Limited U.S. enrollment (1 subject) affecting generalizability

Positives: Primary endpoint met: SUL-DUR noninferior to colistin for 28-day mortality; Upper bound of 95% CI (3.5%) well below 20% margin, would satisfy a 10% margin; Numerically lower mortality (19% vs 32.3%) and favorable secondary endpoints; High unmet medical need for CRABC with limited treatment options; No unexpected safety signals identified in development program
